description: Implement the station power grid system where each room has a power state (powered, backup, dark) that affects lighting, door behavior, and security system activation. The player can interact with power terminals to reroute power between areas.
acceptance_criteria: Each room in the generated sector has a power state: 'powered', 'backup', or 'dark'.
Powered rooms have active overhead lighting, working automatic doors, and active security systems (turrets, cameras).
Dark rooms have no ambient light — only the player's flashlight illuminates them. Automatic doors are stuck closed (must be forced or hacked).
Given a player interacts with a power terminal, when they choose to reroute power, then they can toggle power on/off for connected rooms (powering one room may de-power another).
Room visual treatment changes based on power state: warm amber when powered, near-black when dark, as defined in spec A.3.2.
The minimap shows room power states with color coding (amber = powered, gray = dark).
notes: Spec: 'Powered areas: working doors, lights, turrets (hostile), vending machines, elevators. Dark areas: enemies have advantage, but security systems are offline.' This creates the core risk/reward: power on = see but face turrets, power off = dark but turrets dormant.
